home.heading: Hi, I'm Tyler
content: I'm a front-end engineer with 20+ years of experience building web applications for companies like Vanguard, Bank of America, and Best Egg. I currently lead an engineering team at Best Egg.

When I'm not coding, I'm usually behind a drum kit. I've toured nationally with a major-label band, played thousands of gigs, and I'm now exploring ways to share what I've learned about drumming with others.

home.heading: Engineering
content: I build front-end applications with Angular, Vue.js, and TypeScript. I've worked on design systems, authentication and authorization systems, payment processing interfaces for financial transactions, email campaigns, real-time instrument control interfaces, and customer-facing applications serving millions of users.
My focus is on building maintainable systems, mentoring engineers, and solving complex technical problems.
View my full resume →

home.heading: Music
content: I've been playing drums for most of my life. The Crash Motive (Wind-up Records/Sony BMG) got placements in the Super Bowl, Madden, and The Fantastic Four. I also co-founded SpeakerCity, a top-40/wedding band that became one of the busiest acts in the Mid-Atlantic region.
More about my music →

home.heading: Beyond work
content: I'm a father of two, an avid cyclist and runner, a craft beer enthusiast, and a vinyl collector. I graduated from the University of Delaware with a degree in Business Administration (Finance/Accounting).
I'm based in Kennett Square, Pennsylvania.

resume.heading: Resume
content: 20+ years of experience building web applications for companies like Best Egg, Vanguard, Bank of America, Agilent Technologies, DuPont, and Chemours.

resume.heading: Best Egg
resume.subheading: Senior Lead Software Engineer II · 2024 - Present
content: Lead a cross-functional engineering team responsible for customer-facing applications in verifications, servicing, and authentication serving millions of users.
• Mentor engineers through hands-on guidance, code reviews, and knowledge-sharing
• Develop customer-facing features with A/B testing integration for data-driven experimentation
• Implement comprehensive observability through enterprise events, FullStory, and DataDog
• Establish engineering standards and architectural patterns
• Drive adoption of AI-assisted development tools like Cursor with Claude
• Recognized with Golden Egg Award for "BE a Builder of Great Teams" (Q3 2023)
Tech: Angular, TypeScript, Jest, Karma, GitHub Actions, Node.js, Storybook, DataDog, FullStory

resume.heading: Vanguard
content: Senior technical contributor for Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services design system, supporting thousands of financial advisors serving millions of clients.
• Drove technical implementation of FAS design system through division-wide rebrand

• Architected web component solution with Angular-compatible custom element wrapper, enabling cross-framework integration (Vue, Angular, React, Adobe Experience Manager) and eliminating framework-specific support teams
• Architected and developed Vue.js/NestJS application prototype that became the standard foundation for all FAS single-page applications
• Presented integration strategy to IT leadership and drove adoption across three Angular development teams
• Implemented CI/CD deployment infrastructure for internal documentation using AWS services
Tech: Vue.js, NestJS, Web Components, Storybook, Angular, Custom Elements, AWS, CI/CD

resume.heading: Technical Writers, Inc.
content: Served as on-site front-end specialist consulting with Agilent Technologies's R&D software team to develop instrument control interfaces for global product lines.
• Developed web-based instrument control UI for Agilent's flagship Gas Chromatography hardware using Sencha/Ext JS, implementing real-time monitoring and control through WebSocket connections
• Created data visualization features including live instrument data graphing and visual hardware configuration displays
• Designed custom SVG icons and CSS styling aligned with Agilent's product design standards

• Architected automated build system for Adobe RoboHelp documentation delivery across Gas Chromatography, Mass Spectrometry, and Sampler product lines, supporting 8+ languages with dynamic template generation, theming, and command-line automation for multi-language localization
Tech: JavaScript, Sencha/Ext JS, HTML5, CSS/SCSS, SVG, WebSockets, Adobe RoboHelp, Command-line scripting

resume.heading: Trellist Marketing and Technology
content: Served in multiple technical roles for Fortune 500 and enterprise clients.
Front-End Development:
• Developed responsive websites and landing pages for enterprise clients like Bank of America, David's Bridal, Chemours, and Blue Cross Blue Shield Global, reaching millions of users
• Established front-end development standards and best practices across the digital team
Email Development:

• Architected and developed "adaptive" HTML email campaigns for Bank of America's Loyalty, Cash Rewards, and Travel Rewards programs, reaching millions of customers with full cross-platform compatibility
• Led technical strategy for email development, optimizing for deliverability, accessibility, and cross-client consistency
Systems Management:
• Served as Systems Owner for DuPont's SharePoint security auditing system, delivering weekly stakeholder reports and managing server migration with IBM consultants
SharePoint Development:
• Developed SharePoint intranet solutions for Chemours and Everything But Water
• Coordinated time-sensitive deployments of highly sensitive internal communications with Chemours' communications leadership, requiring off-hours execution
Tech: HTML5, CSS/SCSS, JavaScript, jQuery, SharePoint, Responsive Design, Email Development

resume.heading: Technical Writers, Inc.
content: 11-year consulting role providing technical writing and web development services, primarily for Agilent Technologies. Full-time 2003-2005 and 2007-2014, part-time 2005-2007 while pursuing music full-time with The Crash Motive.
Front-End Developer / Senior Technical Writer (Agilent, 2007-2014):
• Designed standalone web-based document delivery applications distributed via USB/DVD for global product lines
• Architected custom HTML templates and automated build systems for Adobe RoboHelp, streamlining multi-product documentation delivery
• Authored technical documentation using Adobe FrameMaker and converted to web-ready HTML
• Created software UI icons, technical diagrams, and visual assets for R&D teams
Technical Writer / Web Developer (Part-time, 2005-2007):
• Maintained part-time work while touring nationally with The Crash Motive (Wind-up Records)
• Provided ongoing documentation and web development support
Technical Writer / Web Developer (2003-2005):
• Produced regulatory documentation and compliance materials for pharmaceutical clients
• Developed and maintained corporate web presence
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Adobe RoboHelp, Adobe FrameMaker, Adobe Illustrator, Adobe Photoshop

resume.heading: The Crash Motive
content: Co-founding member and drummer for The Crash Motive (formerly Omnisoul), a nationally touring original rock band. Grew the business from independent operation to major label partnership, ultimately signing comprehensive recording, distribution, touring, merchandising, and publishing deals with Wind-up Records and Sony BMG.
• Performed drum parts on studio recordings and arranged drum compositions for albums and singles
• Toured nationally and performed extensively at major venues, opening for nationally recognized acts
• Performed at high-profile industry showcases with globally recognized artists and music executives
• Developed and executed targeted digital marketing campaigns using MySpace's location and interest-based features to drive tour attendance and radio engagement in key markets
• Secured high-profile music placements including Super Bowl XLII (Fox), Madden NFL 2007 (EA Sports), and The Fantastic Four movie/soundtrack (20th Century Fox)
• Licensed music for use in 30+ professional sports stadiums (NFL, MLB, NCAA, NHL)
• Placed music in network television programming including NBC, FOX, and CBS promotional campaigns (2006-2008), 2008 Olympic Trials (NBC), NFL Live, NFL Rewind, and Baseball Tonight (ESPN)
• Negotiated and managed business partnerships with major label executives, booking agents, and licensing representatives

resume.heading: Wilmington Trust Company
resume.subheading: IT Division Intranet Webmaster & IT Service Tech · 2000 - 2003
content: Started as an intern for the Online Portfolio Group (2000-2001) while completing undergraduate studies, then brought on full-time as IT Division Intranet Webmaster and first-level IT service tech (2001-2003).
IT Division Intranet Webmaster (2001-2003):
• Redesigned and developed the IT Service & Support Division intranet website in Flash
• Designed and developed the Online Portfolio Trust Management Group intranet site
• Designed and developed a custom call logging front-end application with Microsoft Access, Visual Basic and SQL Server, used in production across multiple service and support departments
• Provided first-level internal tech support for software, network accounts, technical issues, and hardware failures
Intern for Online Portfolio Group (2000-2001):
• Supported the Online Portfolio Group with production Microsoft Access call logging application and web development projects while completing undergraduate studies
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Flash, Microsoft Access, Visual Basic, SQL Server

music.heading: Music
content: Music has been a big part of my life. This page serves as a place to archive past and present projects.

music.heading: The Crash Motive (2002-2008)
content: We built this band from the ground up and signed with Wind-up Records (Sony BMG distribution). We recorded an album at Avatar Studios in NYC in 2005 and landed some really cool placements including:
• Super Bowl XLII (Fox)
• Madden NFL 2007 (EA Sports)
• The Fantastic Four movie and soundtrack (20th Century Fox)
• Licensed for 30+ NFL, MLB, NCAA, and NHL stadiums
• Network TV: NBC, FOX, CBS promotional campaigns; 2008 Olympic Trials; ESPN programming

music.heading: The Healthy Doses (1999-2000)
content: The Healthy Doses was a jam band based at the University of Delaware. We played locally often (sometimes twice a week at the Deer Park Tavern in Newark, Delaware) and at larger festivals along the East Coast including Phish's Camp Oswego festival in 1999.
